content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA, Output the sum of all elements in AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN denoting the number of elements in AAA.
the second line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
Output a single integer, sum of all the elements in the array AAA.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of the N integers of that test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. the array [8,2,4,1,4] sums to 8+2+4+1+4 = 19.
Single test case, sum of [-1,0,1] is 0.

solution.approach: For each test case we simply iterate over the N numbers, accumulating their total in a 64‑bit variable (long) to avoid overflow. The algorithm is linear in the size of the input and uses only constant extra space. This is optimal because every element must be read at least once to compute the sum.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
